skill.name: java-unit-test
skill.description: Generate maintainable Java unit tests with JUnit 5 and Mockito guidance.
skill.summary: Helps create focused Java unit tests for services, branches, boundaries, and dependency interactions.
skill.author: gavin-ali

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged dynamic import text, weak cryptography, and high entropy in SKILL.md. Review found these are false positives from a markdown import-order bullet, front matter text, and Chinese documentation content, with no executable code or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Dynamic Import Expression
low_findings.description: The static scripts finding points to an import-order guideline that mentions static import. It is markdown documentation, not executable JavaScript or dynamic code loading.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 57 is part of a numbered list for Java import ordering. No import() expression or executable script is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static cryptography finding points to the skill description line. No cryptographic function, hash algorithm, or security-sensitive operation appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains Chinese natural-language metadata describing Java unit test generation. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or related weak cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy Content
low_findings.description: The entropy heuristic appears to be caused by normal markdown front matter and multilingual text. No binary blob, encrypted payload, encoded command, or hidden script was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is readable markdown from line 1 onward. Manual review found structured instructions only, with no obfuscated payload or execution path.

content.value_statement: Java teams often miss branch, boundary, and dependency behavior in unit tests. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create structured JUnit 5 and Mockito tests.
actual_capabilities: Identifies the system under test and its public methods.
Maps method inputs, returns, exceptions, branches, and boundary cases.
Suggests JUnit 5 and Mockito test structure for Java classes.
Guides dependency mocking, stubbing, verification, and argument capture.
Recommends test data builders or helper methods for readable tests.
Covers exception paths, null values, empty collections, and edge cases.
limitations: It does not run tests or measure coverage by itself.
It needs source files and project conventions for accurate output.
It focuses on unit tests, not full integration or end-to-end testing.
It may need adjustment for unusual frameworks or legacy test setups.

use_cases.title: Add Tests for a Service Class
use_cases.description: Generate focused unit tests for public service methods, branches, and dependency interactions.

use_cases.title: Improve Coverage Before a Release
use_cases.description: Find missing boundary and exception cases that should be covered before merging.

use_cases.title: Standardize Test Style
use_cases.description: Create tests that follow project naming, import, assertion, and mocking conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Unit Tests
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one Java class.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate JUnit 5 unit tests for this Java class. Use Mockito only where dependencies require it.

prompt_templates.title: Cover Branches and Boundaries
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request focused on behavior coverage.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this class and create tests for happy paths, branches, null values, empty inputs, and exception cases.

prompt_templates.title: Match Project Test Style
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with existing project conventions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate tests that match the existing test style in this project. Follow current naming, assertions, mocks, and helper patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Refine Coverage Gaps
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this coverage report and source class to add only meaningful missing unit tests. Avoid brittle implementation-detail assertions.

output_examples.input: A Java service class with repository and client dependencies.
output_examples.output: A test plan covering success, missing data, dependency failure, and verification of repository and client calls.

output_examples.input: A method with validation rules and boundary conditions.
output: A list of valid, invalid, null, empty, and extreme input cases.
Recommended assertions for return values and thrown exceptions.

output_examples.input: Existing tests that use AssertJ and Mockito.
output_examples.output: New test cases that follow the same assertion style, mock setup pattern, and naming convention.

best_practices: Provide the source class, direct dependencies, and any existing tests.
Ask for branch, boundary, exception, and dependency interaction coverage.
Review generated tests for business intent before adding them to the project.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for tests without sharing the class under test.
Do not overuse mocks for pure business logic that can be tested directly.
Do not assert private methods or unstable implementation details.

faq.question: Which Java test frameworks does this skill target?
faq.answer: It primarily targets JUnit 5 and Mockito, while following existing project conventions when provided.

faq.question: Can it work with JUnit 4?
faq.answer: Yes, but you should state that the project uses JUnit 4 so the generated tests match that style.

faq.question: Does it execute the generated tests?
faq.answer: No. It provides test design and code guidance, but execution depends on your local build tools.

faq.question: Can it improve coverage reports?
faq.answer: It can suggest missing meaningful tests when you provide the source code and coverage gaps.

faq.question: Does it require Spring Boot?
faq.answer: No. It prefers plain Mockito unit tests and suggests Spring test support only when needed.

faq.question: How should I get the best results?
faq.answer: Share the class under test, dependencies, existing test examples, and any coverage or framework constraints.
